test.command: set -e; ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then (cd "$ROOT/apps/mobile" && flutter test --concurrency=5); fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo test --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --jobs 5; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild test -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS'; fi

typecheck.command: ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then flutter analyze "$ROOT/apps/mobile"; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo check --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --all-targets; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS' build >/dev/null; fi

lint.command: ROOT="/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion"; if [ ! -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ] && [ ! -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ] && [ ! -d "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell" ]; then echo "workspace not scaffolded yet"; exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml" ]; then flutter analyze "$ROOT/apps/mobile"; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" ]; then cargo fmt --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --all --check && cargo clippy --manifest-path "$ROOT/Cargo.toml" --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; fi; if [ -f "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj" ]; then xcodebuild -project "$ROOT/apps/mac-shell/CodexMobileCompanion.xcodeproj" -scheme CodexMobileCompanion -destination 'platform=macOS' build >/dev/null; fi

blockingIssues.description: The desktop QR entrypoint derives `bridge_id` from the bind host/port and advertises `http://127.0.0.1:3110` in the QR payload, so default-config Macs share the same pseudo-identity and scanned phones are pointed at localhost instead of a reachable private bridge route.

The mobile pairing flow originally finalized trust entirely in local storage, restored paired state without a fail-closed bridge handshake, and only enforced reused-session/private-route checks at scan time with ephemeral client state.

The shared bridge startup path still falls back to localhost QR base URLs because it does not pass `--pairing-base-url`, and persisted consumed session IDs can collide with reset `pairing-session-N` sequences after a bridge restart, breaking legitimate future re-pair flows.

`POST /policy/access-mode` updates the global mode with no trust/session/authorization check, allowing any reachable client to escalate itself to `full_control` and bypass the intended approval model.

appliedUpdates.description: Expanded `.factory/library/api.md` with the concrete pairing QR payload fields, finalize/handshake request-response contracts, policy/approval/security endpoints, and shared security event record shape exposed by the bridge.

Expanded `.factory/library/security.md` with the device-unique bridge identity invariant, private-route QR requirement, bridge-owned finalize/session-token trust semantics, and handshake fail-closed expectations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the minimum manual verification expected for early pairing-only shell slices so workers do not mark the full shell-state checklist as completed when only QR generation or liveness was actually exercised.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `desktop-pairing-qr-entrypoint` handoff recorded only a liveness/manual sanity check, while the current desktop-worker skill still reads like the worker must verify unpaired, paired-idle, paired-active, and bridge-offline shell states for every desktop feature.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document when non-interactive Exec runs may substitute comprehensive automated coverage for manual simulator/device verification, and require workers to record what evidence they used for permission/error-state coverage in that case.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `mobile-qr-pairing-and-trust-confirmation` worker explicitly deviated from the manual verification step because Exec mode is non-interactive, but the current mobile-worker skill does not explain the acceptable substitute or evidence standard.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require security workers to either execute and record the manual fail-closed verification step or explicitly mark the deviation when only automated checks were run; do not allow `followedProcedure: true` when the user-visible fail-closed walkthrough was skipped.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `security-trust-registry-and-private-handshake` and `security-policy-engine-and-access-mode-foundations` handoffs marked `followedProcedure` true even though their review evidence shows the manual fail-closed/user-visible verification step was not actually performed.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared bridge service in `.factory/services.yaml` should be updated to pass `--pairing-base-url` so the canonical startup path emits private pairing URLs.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected because fixing the existing bridge command is a non-additive service change and should be handled by a dedicated fix feature alongside the underlying code issues, not by the validator.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.factory/library/security.md` was missing the concrete long-lived bridge-identity invariant for pairing trust.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented via the applied security library update in this scrutiny round.

rejectedObservations.observation: `.factory/library/api.md` was too high-level to capture the concrete pairing, policy, approval-resolution, and security-event contracts now exposed by the bridge.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented via the applied API library update in this scrutiny round.
